107ページ。 社地は「茶屋の森」と呼ばれる式内社研究会 編（1990）：260ページ。社地の[[面積]]は370.87[[坪]]（≒1,226[[平方メートル|m2]]）式内社研究会 編（1990）：265ページ。『皇大神宮儀式帳』に「坐地大土神社之[[四至]]内」とあり、[[平安時代]]より大土御祖神社と同じ地に鎮座していたことが窺える。 2社は同座しており、[[社殿]]は1つである。[[神明造]][[板葺]]の社殿は[[南]]の方角を向いており、一重の[[玉垣]]に囲まれている式内社研究会 編（1990）：275ページ。[[賽銭]]箱は置かれていない。『皇大神宮儀式帳』によれば、社殿は板葺ではなく[[茅葺|萱葺]]であったという。 == 国津御祖神社 == 国津御祖神社は、内宮の摂社27社のうち第11位である宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：9 - 11ページ。 [[祭神]]は宇治比賣命（うじひめのみこと）・田村比賣命（たむらひめのみこと）。2柱とも国生（くなり）の神の御子であり、[[産土神]]であるという。田村比賣命を「村田比賣命」と記載する古書も多いが、誤記と見られる。 祭神が国生の神の御子であることから「御子社」の別名がある式内社研究会 編（1990）：272ページ。ほかに用例は少ないものの、「一本社」・「一元社」の異称もある。 == 葦立弖神社 == 葦立弖神社は、内宮の末社16社のうち、第14位である宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：13 - 14ページ。社殿は中絶し、国津御祖神社に同座する宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：14ページ。 祭神は玉移良比女命（たまやらひめのみこと）。宇治都比女命（うじつひめのみこと）の子であるとされ、産土神であるという。宇治都比女命は国津御祖神社の祭神・宇治比賣命と同じであると考えられている式内社研究会 編（1990）：274ページ。 == 歴史 == 伊勢神宮の摂社の定義より『[[延喜式神名帳]]』成立、すなわち[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）以前に創建された伊勢文化舎（2008）：22ページ。『皇大神宮儀式帳』にも「国津御祖社」として記載があることから[[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前から存在したことになる。 [[中世]]以後、祭祀が断絶し、[[寛文]]3年[[12月22日 (旧暦)|12月22日]]（[[グレゴリオ暦]]：[[1664年]][[1月20日]]）に[[遷御]]（せんぎょ）の儀を行い再興された。大土御祖神社の旧社地を特定が容易であった式内社研究会 編（1990）：262ページため、同じ社地の国津御祖神社も労せず旧地に復興された。[[明治4年]]（[[1871年]]）に葦立弖神社を国津御祖神社へ同座させる。 社殿は[[1914年]]（大正3年）に造り替えられた宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：10ページ。45年後の[[1959年]]（昭和34年）7月に建て替えられ、[[1982年]]（昭和57年）10月に大修繕が施された。 == 祭祀 == 国津御祖神社で行われる祭祀は、大土御祖神社と同じである式内社研究会 編（1990）：275ページ。すなわち、巡回祭典の形で[[祈年祭]]（2月）、[[月次祭]]（6月・12月）、[[神嘗祭]]（10月）、[[新嘗祭]]（11月）の際は社殿の前で祭祀が行われ、[[歳旦祭]]（1月）、[[元始祭]]（1月）、建国記念祭（[[2月11日]]）、風日祈祭（5月・8月）、[[天長祭]]（[[12月23日]]）は遥祀を催行する式内社研究会 編（1990）：263ページ。 == 植物相 == 大土御祖神社と同じ社地に鎮座するため、植物相も同じである。 [[1977年]]（昭和52年）に行われた[[境内]]の[[立木]]調査によると、カヤノキ・[[イヌマキ|マキ]]・スギ・[[ヒノキ]]・[[カシ]]・[[シイ]]・クスノキ・[[ヤブニッケイ]]・[[シロダモ]]・[[タブノキ|タブ]]・[[トベラ]]・[[モチノキ]]・[[ヤブツバキ]]を主要樹種とし、計258本あった。 == 交通 == [[File:Chaya no Mori in Kusube.jpg|thumb|国津御祖神社・葦立弖神社の入り口]] 国津御祖神社・葦立弖神社の東には神宮神田が広がる。付近には[[伊勢市立四郷小学校]]、伊勢市役所四郷支所、櫲樟尾神社（くすおじんじゃ）がある。 * [[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[近鉄鳥羽線|鳥羽線]][[五十鈴川駅]]より ** 徒歩約13分（約1[[キロメートル|km]]）。 ** [[伊勢市コミュニティバス]]「おかげバス」乗車、「四郷小学校前」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、徒歩約1分（約150m）。 * [[伊勢自動車道]][[伊勢インターチェンジ|伊勢IC]]より[[国道23号]]を南下、「楠部」[[交差点]]を左折、[[三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線]]を東へ約1分（約750m）。付近に[[駐車場]]はない。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == 関連項目 == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0306.html 国津御祖神社（くにつみおやじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（葦立弖神社の説明を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|くにつみおやしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:国津御祖神社]][[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:unsorted completion categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[国津御祖神社]] to [[Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]]
revision.text: #REDIRECT [[Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]]

{{Shrine |name= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Kunitsu Mioya & Ashidate shrine.jpg|270px]] |location= {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg= 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.5 |longitude_deg= 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.4 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: Ujihime-no-Mikoto, Tamurahime-no-Mikoto
Ashidate Shrine: Tamairahime-no-Mikoto |rank= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} Subordinate Shrine
Ashidate Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |other_names= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: Mikosha (御子社) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }} '''Kunitsu Mioya Shrine''' (国津御祖神社, *Kunitsu Mioya Jinja*) is a {{ill|subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}). This article also covers '''Ashidate Shrine''' (葦立弖神社, *Ashidate Jinja*), which is co-located with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.107. [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[ja:国津御祖神社]] == Overview == Located at 2132 Ozaki, Kusube-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture,Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.273 Kunitsu Mioya Shrine (国津御祖神社) and Ashitate Shrine (葦立弖神社) are situated further along the approach to {{ill|Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社}} near the Isuzubashi Bridge.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.106-107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.260 The site covers an area of approximately 1,226 square meters.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.265 According to the "Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō," both shrines have coexisted at the same location since the Heian period. Both shrines share a single honden (main hall), built in the shinmei-zukuri style with a wooden roof, facing south and surrounded by a tamagaki (sacred fence).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.275 No offertory box is present. Historical records indicate that the roof was originally thatched. == Kunitsu Mioya Shrine == Ranked 11th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 The enshrined deities are Ujihime-no-Mikoto (宇治比賣命) and Tamurahime-no-Mikoto (田村比賣命), both children of the deity Kunari and considered guardian deities. Due to their lineage, the shrine is also known as "Miko-sha" (御子社), with occasional references as "Ippon-sha" or "Ichigen-sha."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.272 == Ashitate Shrine == Ranked 14th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine shares the honden with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.14 The enshrined deity is Tamayara-hime-no-Mikoto (玉移良比女命), believed to be the child of Ujitsu-hime-no-Mikoto and also considered a guardian deity. == History == The shrine was founded before 927, as it is listed in the "Engishiki Jinmyōchō."Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 The "Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō" also mentions it as "Kunitsu Mioya-sha," indicating its existence before 804. After falling into disuse during the medieval period, it was restored in 1664 through the sengyo (relocation of the deity) ceremony. In 1871, Ashitate Shrine was officially integrated with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine. The honden was rebuilt in 1914, renovated in 1959, and underwent major repairs in 1982. == Festivals == Festivals at Kunitsu Mioya Shrine are conducted jointly with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine. Ceremonies include the Kinensai (February), Tsukinamisai (June and December), Kannamesai (October), and Niinamesai (November), held in front of the honden. Remote ceremonies are conducted for the Saitan-sai (January), Genshi-sai (January), National Foundation Day (February 11), Kazahi-no-Matsuri (May and August), and Tenchō-sai (December 23).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.263 == Flora == Sharing the grounds with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine, the flora is identical. A 1977 survey recorded 258 trees, including kaya, maki, cedar, cypress, oak, camellia, camphor, and others. == Access == Located east of Kunitsu Mioya Shrine and Ashitate Shrine are the sacred rice fields of the Grand Shrine. Nearby landmarks include Ise City Yotsugo Elementary School and Kusuo Shrine. * **By Train:** 13-minute walk from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line (approx. 1 km). * **By Bus:** 1-minute walk from the "Yotsugo Elementary School" stop on the "Okage Bus." * **By Car:** 1-minute drive (approx. 750 m) from the "Kusube" intersection via National Route 23 after exiting the Ise IC on the Ise Expressway. No parking available. == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == 関連項目 == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0306.html 国津御祖神社（くにつみおやじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（葦立弖神社の説明を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|くにつみおやしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:国津御祖神社]][[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:unsorted completion categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]]

{{Shrine |name= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Kunitsu Mioya & Ashidate shrine.jpg|270px]] |location= {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg= 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.5 |longitude_deg= 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.4 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: Ujihime-no-Mikoto, Tamurahime-no-Mikoto
Ashidate Shrine: Tamairahime-no-Mikoto |rank= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} Subordinate Shrine
Ashidate Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |other_names= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: Mikosha (御子社) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }} '''Kunitsu Mioya Shrine''' (国津御祖神社, *Kunitsu Mioya Jinja*) is a {{ill|subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}). This article also covers '''Ashidate Shrine''' (葦立弖神社, *Ashidate Jinja*), which is co-located with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.107. [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[ja:国津御祖神社]] == Overview == {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社}} and {{ill|Ashitate Shrine|ja|葦立弖神社}} are located at 2132, Oazaki, Kusube-cho, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.273 They are situated further along the approach of {{ill|Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社}} and {{ill|Uji no Nukigami Shrine|ja|宇治乃奴鬼神社}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.106-107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.260 The area spans approximately 1,226 m².Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.265 According to the *Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō*, these shrines have been located here since the Heian period. Both shrines share a single {{ill|Shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with wooden boards, facing south, and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.275 There is no offertory box. Historically, the building was thatched. == Kunitsu Mioya Shrine == Ranked 11th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Naikū|ja|内宮}}.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Uji-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇治比賣命}} and {{ill|Tamura-hime no Mikoto|ja|田村比賣命}}. Both are children of the {{ill|Kuninushi no Kami|ja|国生の神}} and are regarded as local tutelary deities. Due to their divine lineage, the shrine is also called "Miko-sha" (御子社), meaning "Shrine of the Divine Children."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.272 == Ashitate Shrine == Ranked 14th among the 16 subordinate shrines of Naikū.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine building no longer exists, and the deity is enshrined together with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.14 The enshrined deity is {{ill|Tamayara-hime no Mikoto|ja|玉移良比女命}}, considered a child of {{ill|Ujitsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇治都比女命}} and a local tutelary deity. == History == Established before 927 CE, as mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It is also listed in the *Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō*, confirming its existence before 804 CE. Worship ceased in the medieval period, but the shrines were restored on December 22, 1664 (Gregorian: January 20, 1665). Since the original site of Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine was easy to locate,Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.262 both shrines were re-established at their original locations. In 1871, Ashitate Shrine was formally merged with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine. The shrine building was reconstructed in 1914, rebuilt in 1959, and extensively renovated in 1982. == Rituals == Rituals are performed similarly to those at Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine. These include: * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February) * {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}} (June and December) * {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) Other rites such as {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}}, {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}}, National Foundation Day (February 11), {{ill|Kaze no Hi no Matsuri|ja|風日祈祭}} (May, August), and {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭}} (December 23) are conducted remotely.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.263 == Flora == Since it shares the grounds with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine, the plant life is identical. A 1977 survey found 258 trees, including Kaya, {{ill|Inumaki|ja|イヌマキ}}, Cryptomeria, Hinoki, Oak, Shii, Camphor, Yabu-nikkei, Shirodamo, Tabu, Tobera, Mochi, and Yabutsubaki. == Access == [[File:Chaya no Mori in Kusube.jpg|thumb|Entrance to Kunitsu Mioya Shrine and Ashitate Shrine]] * **By Train:** From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} (Kintetsu Toba Line) * 13-minute walk (~1 km) * Take the {{ill|Ise Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス}} "Okage Bus," alight at "Shigo Elementary School" stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m) * **By Car:** From {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}} (Ise Expressway), drive south on National Route 23, turn left at "Kusube Intersection," and continue east on Prefectural Route 37 for ~750 m. No parking is available. == Related Pages == * {{ill|Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社}} * {{ill|Uji no Nukigami Shrine|ja|宇治乃奴鬼神社}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:国津御祖神社]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == 関連項目 == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0306.html 国津御祖神社（くにつみおやじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（葦立弖神社の説明を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|くにつみおやしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:国津御祖神社]][[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:unsorted completion categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]]

{{Shrine |name= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Kunitsu Mioya & Ashidate shrine.jpg|270px]] |location= {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg= 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.5 |longitude_deg= 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.4 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: Ujihime-no-Mikoto, Tamurahime-no-Mikoto
Ashidate Shrine: Tamairahime-no-Mikoto |rank= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} Subordinate Shrine
Ashidate Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |other_names= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: Mikosha (御子社) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }} '''Kunitsu Mioya Shrine''' (国津御祖神社, *Kunitsu Mioya Jinja*) is a {{ill|subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}). This article also covers '''Ashidate Shrine''' (葦立弖神社, *Ashidate Jinja*), which is co-located with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.107. [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[ja:国津御祖神社]] == Overview == {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社}} and {{ill|Ashitate Shrine|ja|葦立弖神社}} are located at 2132, Oazaki, Kusube-cho, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.273 They are situated further along the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社}} and {{ill|Uji no Nukigami Shrine|ja|宇治乃奴鬼神社}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.106-107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.260 The area spans approximately 1,226 m².Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.265 According to the *Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō*, these shrines have been located here since the Heian period. Both shrines share a single {{ill|Shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with wooden boards, facing south, and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.275 There is no offertory box. Historically, the building was thatched. == Kunitsu Mioya Shrine == Ranked 11th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Naikū|ja|内宮}}.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Uji-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇治比賣命}} and {{ill|Tamura-hime no Mikoto|ja|田村比賣命}}. Both are children of the {{ill|Kuninushi no Kami|ja|国生の神}} and are regarded as local tutelary deities. Due to their divine lineage, the shrine is also called "Miko-sha" (御子社), meaning "Shrine of the Divine Children."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.272 == Ashitate Shrine == Ranked 14th among the 16 subordinate shrines of Naikū.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine building no longer exists, and the deity is enshrined together with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.14 The enshrined deity is {{ill|Tamayara-hime no Mikoto|ja|玉移良比女命}}, considered a child of {{ill|Ujitsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇治都比女命}} and a local tutelary deity. == History == Established before 927 CE, as mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It is also listed in the *Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō*, confirming its existence before 804 CE. Worship ceased in the medieval period, but the shrines were restored on December 22, 1664 (Gregorian: January 20, 1665). Since the original site of Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine was easy to locate,Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.262 both shrines were re-established at their original locations. In 1871, Ashitate Shrine was formally merged with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine. The shrine building was reconstructed in 1914, rebuilt in 1959, and extensively renovated in 1982. == Rituals == Rituals are performed similarly to those at Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine. These include: * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February) * {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}} (June and December) * {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) Other rites such as {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}}, {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}}, National Foundation Day (February 11), {{ill|Kaze no Hi no Matsuri|ja|風日祈祭}} (May, August), and {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭}} (December 23) are conducted remotely.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.263 == Flora == Since it shares the grounds with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine, the plant life is identical. A 1977 survey found 258 trees, including Kaya, {{ill|Inumaki|ja|イヌマキ}}, Cryptomeria, Hinoki, Oak, Shii, Camphor, Yabu-nikkei, Shirodamo, Tabu, Tobera, Mochi, and Yabutsubaki. == Access == [[File:Chaya no Mori in Kusube.jpg|thumb|Entrance to Kunitsu Mioya Shrine and Ashitate Shrine]] * **By Train:** From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} (Kintetsu Toba Line) * 13-minute walk (~1 km) * Take the {{ill|Ise Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス}} "Okage Bus," alight at "Shigo Elementary School" stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m) * **By Car:** From {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}} (Ise Expressway), drive south on National Route 23, turn left at "Kusube Intersection," and continue east on Prefectural Route 37 for ~750 m. No parking is available. == Related Pages == * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社}} * {{ill|Uji no Nukigami Shrine|ja|宇治乃奴鬼神社}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:国津御祖神社]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == 関連項目 == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0306.html 国津御祖神社（くにつみおやじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（葦立弖神社の説明を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|くにつみおやしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:国津御祖神社]][[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:unsorted completion categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]]

In 1871, Ashitate Shrine was formally merged with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine. The shrine building was reconstructed in 1914, rebuilt in 1959, and extensively renovated in 1982. == Rituals == Rituals are performed similarly to those at Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine. These include: * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February) * {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}} (June and December) * {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) Other rites such as {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}}, {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}}, National Foundation Day (February 11), {{ill|Kaze no Hi no Matsuri|ja|風日祈祭}} (May, August), and {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭}} (December 23) are conducted remotely.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.263 == Flora == Since it shares the grounds with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine, the plant life is identical. A 1977 survey found 258 trees, including Kaya, {{ill|Inumaki|ja|イヌマキ}}, Cryptomeria, Hinoki, Oak, Shii, Camphor, Yabu-nikkei, Shirodamo, Tabu, Tobera, Mochi, and Yabutsubaki. == Access == [[File:Chaya no Mori in Kusube.jpg|thumb|Entrance to Kunitsu Mioya Shrine and Ashitate Shrine]] * **By Train:** From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} (Kintetsu Toba Line) * 13-minute walk (~1 km) * Take the {{ill|Ise Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス}} "Okage Bus," alight at "Shigo Elementary School" stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m) * **By Car:** From {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}} (Ise Expressway), drive south on National Route 23, turn left at "Kusube Intersection," and continue east on Prefectural Route 37 for ~750 m. No parking is available. == Related Pages == * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|lt=Uji-no-Nuki Shrine|lt_ja=宇治乃奴鬼神社}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:国津御祖神社]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == 関連項目 == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0306.html 国津御祖神社（くにつみおやじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（葦立弖神社の説明を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|くにつみおやしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:国津御祖神社]][[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:unsorted completion categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Kunitsu Mioya & Ashidate shrine.jpg|270px]] |location= {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg= 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.5 |longitude_deg= 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.4 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: Ujihime-no-Mikoto, Tamurahime-no-Mikoto
Ashidate Shrine: Tamairahime-no-Mikoto |rank= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} Subordinate Shrine
Ashidate Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |other_names= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: Mikosha (御子社) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }} '''Kunitsu Mioya Shrine''' (国津御祖神社, *Kunitsu Mioya Jinja*) is a {{ill|subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}). This article also covers '''Ashidate Shrine''' (葦立弖神社, *Ashidate Jinja*), which is co-located with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.107. [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[ja:国津御祖神社]] == Overview == {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社}} and {{ill|Ashitate Shrine|ja|葦立弖神社}} are located at 2132, Oazaki, Kusube-cho, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.273 They are situated further along the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社}} and {{ill|Uji no Nukigami Shrine|ja|宇治乃奴鬼神社}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.106-107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.260 The area spans approximately 1,226 m².Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.265 According to the *Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō*, these shrines have been located here since the Heian period. Both shrines share a single {{ill|Shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with wooden boards, facing south, and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.275 There is no offertory box. Historically, the building was thatched. == Kunitsu Mioya Shrine == Ranked 11th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Naikū|ja|内宮}}.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Uji-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇治比賣命}} and {{ill|Tamura-hime no Mikoto|ja|田村比賣命}}. Both are children of the {{ill|Kuninushi no Kami|ja|国生の神}} and are regarded as local tutelary deities. Due to their divine lineage, the shrine is also called "Miko-sha" (御子社), meaning "Shrine of the Divine Children."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.272 == Ashitate Shrine == Ranked 14th among the 16 subordinate shrines of Naikū.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine building no longer exists, and the deity is enshrined together with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.14 The enshrined deity is {{ill|Tamayara-hime no Mikoto|ja|玉移良比女命}}, considered a child of {{ill|Ujitsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇治都比女命}} and a local tutelary deity. == History == Established before 927 CE, as mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It is also listed in the *Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō*, confirming its existence before 804 CE. Worship ceased in the medieval period, but the shrines were restored on December 22, 1664 (Gregorian: January 20, 1665). Since the original site of Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine was easy to locate,Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.262 both shrines were re-established at their original locations. In 1871, Ashitate Shrine was formally merged with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine. The shrine building was reconstructed in 1914, rebuilt in 1959, and extensively renovated in 1982. == Rituals == Rituals are performed similarly to those at Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine. These include: * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February) * {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}} (June and December) * {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) Other rites such as {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}}, {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}}, National Foundation Day (February 11), {{ill|Kaze no Hi no Matsuri|ja|風日祈祭}} (May, August), and {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭}} (December 23) are conducted remotely.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.263 == Flora == Since it shares the grounds with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine, the plant life is identical. A 1977 survey found 258 trees, including Kaya, {{ill|Inumaki|ja|イヌマキ}}, Cryptomeria, Hinoki, Oak, Shii, Camphor, Yabu-nikkei, Shirodamo, Tabu, Tobera, Mochi, and Yabutsubaki. == Access == [[File:Chaya no Mori in Kusube.jpg|thumb|Entrance to Kunitsu Mioya Shrine and Ashitate Shrine]] * **By Train:** From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} (Kintetsu Toba Line) * 13-minute walk (~1 km) * Take the {{ill|Ise Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス}} "Okage Bus," alight at "Shigo Elementary School" stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m) * **By Car:** From {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}} (Ise Expressway), drive south on National Route 23, turn left at "Kusube Intersection," and continue east on Prefectural Route 37 for ~750 m. No parking is available. == Related Pages == * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|lt=Uji-no-Nuki Shrine|lt_ja=宇治乃奴鬼神社}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:国津御祖神社]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == 関連項目 == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0306.html 国津御祖神社（くにつみおやじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（葦立弖神社の説明を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|くにつみおやしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine-qq]] [[ja:国津御祖神社]][[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]]

This article also covers '''Ashidate Shrine''' (葦立弖神社, *Ashidate Jinja*), which is co-located with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.107. [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[ja:国津御祖神社]] == Overview == {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社}} and {{ill|Ashitate Shrine|ja|葦立弖神社}} are located at 2132, Oazaki, Kusube-cho, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.273 They are situated further along the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社}} and {{ill|Uji no Nukigami Shrine|ja|宇治乃奴鬼神社}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.106-107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.260 The area spans approximately 1,226 m².Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.265 According to the *Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō*, these shrines have been located here since the Heian period. Both shrines share a single {{ill|Shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with wooden boards, facing south, and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.275 There is no offertory box. Historically, the building was thatched. == Kunitsu Mioya Shrine == Ranked 11th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Naikū|ja|内宮}}.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Uji-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇治比賣命}} and {{ill|Tamura-hime no Mikoto|ja|田村比賣命}}. Both are children of the {{ill|Kuninushi no Kami|ja|国生の神}} and are regarded as local tutelary deities. Due to their divine lineage, the shrine is also called "Miko-sha" (御子社), meaning "Shrine of the Divine Children."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.272 == Ashitate Shrine == Ranked 14th among the 16 subordinate shrines of Naikū.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine building no longer exists, and the deity is enshrined together with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.14 The enshrined deity is {{ill|Tamayara-hime no Mikoto|ja|玉移良比女命}}, considered a child of {{ill|Ujitsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇治都比女命}} and a local tutelary deity. == History == Established before 927 CE, as mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It is also listed in the *Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō*, confirming its existence before 804 CE. Worship ceased in the medieval period, but the shrines were restored on December 22, 1664 (Gregorian: January 20, 1665). Since the original site of Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine was easy to locate,Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.262 both shrines were re-established at their original locations. In 1871, Ashitate Shrine was formally merged with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine. The shrine building was reconstructed in 1914, rebuilt in 1959, and extensively renovated in 1982. == Rituals == Rituals are performed similarly to those at Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine. These include: * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February) * {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}} (June and December) * {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) Other rites such as {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}}, {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}}, National Foundation Day (February 11), {{ill|Kaze no Hi no Matsuri|ja|風日祈祭}} (May, August), and {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭}} (December 23) are conducted remotely.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.263 == Flora == Since it shares the grounds with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine, the plant life is identical. A 1977 survey found 258 trees, including Kaya, {{ill|Inumaki|ja|イヌマキ}}, Cryptomeria, Hinoki, Oak, Shii, Camphor, Yabu-nikkei, Shirodamo, Tabu, Tobera, Mochi, and Yabutsubaki. == Access == [[File:Chaya no Mori in Kusube.jpg|thumb|Entrance to Kunitsu Mioya Shrine and Ashitate Shrine]] * **By Train:** From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} (Kintetsu Toba Line) * 13-minute walk (~1 km) * Take the {{ill|Ise Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス}} "Okage Bus," alight at "Shigo Elementary School" stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m) * **By Car:** From {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}} (Ise Expressway), drive south on National Route 23, turn left at "Kusube Intersection," and continue east on Prefectural Route 37 for ~750 m. No parking is available. == Related Pages == * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|lt=Uji-no-Nuki Shrine|lt_ja=宇治乃奴鬼神社}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:国津御祖神社]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == 関連項目 == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0306.html 国津御祖神社（くにつみおやじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（葦立弖神社の説明を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|くにつみおやしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine-qq]] [[ja:国津御祖神社]][[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == 関連項目 == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0306.html 国津御祖神社（くにつみおやじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（葦立弖神社の説明を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|くにつみおやしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine-qq]] [[ja:国津御祖神社]][[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:ppp]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Kunitsu Mioya & Ashidate shrine.jpg|270px]] |location= {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg= 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.5 |longitude_deg= 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.4 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: Ujihime-no-Mikoto, Tamurahime-no-Mikoto
Ashidate Shrine: Tamairahime-no-Mikoto |rank= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} Subordinate Shrine
Ashidate Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |other_names= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: Mikosha (御子社) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }} '''Kunitsu Mioya Shrine''' (国津御祖神社, *Kunitsu Mioya Jinja*) is a {{ill|subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}). This article also covers '''Ashidate Shrine''' (葦立弖神社, *Ashidate Jinja*), which is co-located with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.107. [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[ja:国津御祖神社]] == Overview == {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社}} and {{ill|Ashitate Shrine|ja|葦立弖神社}} are located at 2132, Oazaki, Kusube-cho, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.273 They are situated further along the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社}} and {{ill|Uji no Nukigami Shrine|ja|宇治乃奴鬼神社}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.106-107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.260 The area spans approximately 1,226 m².Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.265 According to the *Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō*, these shrines have been located here since the Heian period. Both shrines share a single {{ill|Shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with wooden boards, facing south, and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.275 There is no offertory box. Historically, the building was thatched. == Kunitsu Mioya Shrine == Ranked 11th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Naikū|ja|内宮}}.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Uji-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇治比賣命}} and {{ill|Tamura-hime no Mikoto|ja|田村比賣命}}. Both are children of the {{ill|Kuninushi no Kami|ja|国生の神}} and are regarded as local tutelary deities. Due to their divine lineage, the shrine is also called "Miko-sha" (御子社), meaning "Shrine of the Divine Children."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.272 == Ashitate Shrine == Ranked 14th among the 16 subordinate shrines of Naikū.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine building no longer exists, and the deity is enshrined together with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.14 The enshrined deity is {{ill|Tamayara-hime no Mikoto|ja|玉移良比女命}}, considered a child of {{ill|Ujitsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇治都比女命}} and a local tutelary deity. == History == Established before 927 CE, as mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It is also listed in the *Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō*, confirming its existence before 804 CE. Worship ceased in the medieval period, but the shrines were restored on December 22, 1664 (Gregorian: January 20, 1665). Since the original site of Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine was easy to locate,Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.262 both shrines were re-established at their original locations. In 1871, Ashitate Shrine was formally merged with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine. The shrine building was reconstructed in 1914, rebuilt in 1959, and extensively renovated in 1982. == Rituals == Rituals are performed similarly to those at Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine. These include: * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February) * {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}} (June and December) * {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) Other rites such as {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}}, {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}}, National Foundation Day (February 11), {{ill|Kaze no Hi no Matsuri|ja|風日祈祭}} (May, August), and {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭}} (December 23) are conducted remotely.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.263 == Flora == Since it shares the grounds with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine, the plant life is identical. A 1977 survey found 258 trees, including Kaya, {{ill|Inumaki|ja|イヌマキ}}, Cryptomeria, Hinoki, Oak, Shii, Camphor, Yabu-nikkei, Shirodamo, Tabu, Tobera, Mochi, and Yabutsubaki. == Access == [[File:Chaya no Mori in Kusube.jpg|thumb|Entrance to Kunitsu Mioya Shrine and Ashitate Shrine]] * **By Train:** From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} (Kintetsu Toba Line) * 13-minute walk (~1 km) * Take the {{ill|Ise Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス}} "Okage Bus," alight at "Shigo Elementary School" stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m) * **By Car:** From {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}} (Ise Expressway), drive south on National Route 23, turn left at "Kusube Intersection," and continue east on Prefectural Route 37 for ~750 m. No parking is available. == Related Pages == * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|lt=Uji-no-Nuki Shrine|lt_ja=宇治乃奴鬼神社}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:国津御祖神社]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == 関連項目 == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0306.html 国津御祖神社（くにつみおやじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（葦立弖神社の説明を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|くにつみおやしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine-qq]] [[ja:国津御祖神社]][[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:ppp]]

{{Shrine |name= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Kunitsu Mioya & Ashidate shrine.jpg|270px]] |location= {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg= 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.5 |longitude_deg= 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.4 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: Ujihime-no-Mikoto, Tamurahime-no-Mikoto

This article also covers '''Ashidate Shrine''' (葦立弖神社, *Ashidate Jinja*), which is co-located with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.107. [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[ja:国津御祖神社]] == Overview == {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社}} and {{ill|Ashitate Shrine|ja|葦立弖神社}} are located at 2132, Oazaki, Kusube-cho, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.273 They are situated further along the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社}} and {{ill|Uji no Nukigami Shrine|ja|宇治乃奴鬼神社}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.106-107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.260 The area spans approximately 1,226 m².Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.265 According to the *Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō*, these shrines have been located here since the Heian period. Both shrines share a single {{ill|Shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with wooden boards, facing south, and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.275 There is no offertory box. Historically, the building was thatched. == Kunitsu Mioya Shrine == Ranked 11th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Naikū|ja|内宮}}.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Uji-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇治比賣命}} and {{ill|Tamura-hime no Mikoto|ja|田村比賣命}}. Both are children of the {{ill|Kuninushi no Kami|ja|国生の神}} and are regarded as local tutelary deities. Due to their divine lineage, the shrine is also called "Miko-sha" (御子社), meaning "Shrine of the Divine Children."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.272 == Ashitate Shrine == Ranked 14th among the 16 subordinate shrines of Naikū.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine building no longer exists, and the deity is enshrined together with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.14 The enshrined deity is {{ill|Tamayara-hime no Mikoto|ja|玉移良比女命}}, considered a child of {{ill|Ujitsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇治都比女命}} and a local tutelary deity. == History == Established before 927 CE, as mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It is also listed in the *Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō*, confirming its existence before 804 CE. Worship ceased in the medieval period, but the shrines were restored on December 22, 1664 (Gregorian: January 20, 1665). Since the original site of Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine was easy to locate,Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.262 both shrines were re-established at their original locations. In 1871, Ashitate Shrine was formally merged with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine. The shrine building was reconstructed in 1914, rebuilt in 1959, and extensively renovated in 1982. == Rituals == Rituals are performed similarly to those at Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine. These include: * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February) * {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}} (June and December) * {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) Other rites such as {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}}, {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}}, National Foundation Day (February 11), {{ill|Kaze no Hi no Matsuri|ja|風日祈祭}} (May, August), and {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭}} (December 23) are conducted remotely.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.263 == Flora == Since it shares the grounds with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine, the plant life is identical. A 1977 survey found 258 trees, including Kaya, {{ill|Inumaki|ja|イヌマキ}}, Cryptomeria, Hinoki, Oak, Shii, Camphor, Yabu-nikkei, Shirodamo, Tabu, Tobera, Mochi, and Yabutsubaki. == Access == [[File:Chaya no Mori in Kusube.jpg|thumb|Entrance to Kunitsu Mioya Shrine and Ashitate Shrine]] * **By Train:** From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} (Kintetsu Toba Line) * 13-minute walk (~1 km) * Take the {{ill|Ise Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス}} "Okage Bus," alight at "Shigo Elementary School" stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m) * **By Car:** From {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}} (Ise Expressway), drive south on National Route 23, turn left at "Kusube Intersection," and continue east on Prefectural Route 37 for ~750 m. No parking is available. == Related Pages == * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|lt=Uji-no-Nuki Shrine|lt_ja=宇治乃奴鬼神社}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:国津御祖神社]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == 関連項目 == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0306.html 国津御祖神社（くにつみおやじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（葦立弖神社の説明を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|くにつみおやしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine-qq]] [[ja:国津御祖神社]][[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:ppp]] [[ja:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]]

This article also covers '''Ashidate Shrine''' (葦立弖神社, *Ashidate Jinja*), which is co-located with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines share a single {{ill|Shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with wooden boards, facing south, and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.275 There is no offertory box. Both are children of the {{ill|Kuninushi no Kami|ja|国生の神}} and are regarded as local tutelary deities. Due to their divine lineage, the shrine is also called "Miko-sha" (御子社), meaning "Shrine of the Divine Children."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.272 == Ashitate Shrine == Ranked 14th among the 16 subordinate shrines of Naikū.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine building no longer exists, and the deity is enshrined together with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.14 The enshrined deity is {{ill|Tamayara-hime no Mikoto|ja|玉移良比女命}}, considered a child of {{ill|Ujitsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇治都比女命}} and a local tutelary deity. == History == Established before 927 CE, as mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It is also listed in the *Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō*, confirming its existence before 804 CE. Worship ceased in the medieval period, but the shrines were restored on December 22, 1664 (Gregorian: January 20, 1665). Since the original site of Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine was easy to locate,Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.262 both shrines were re-established at their original locations. In 1871, Ashitate Shrine was formally merged with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine. The shrine building was reconstructed in 1914, rebuilt in 1959, and extensively renovated in 1982. == Rituals == Rituals are performed similarly to those at Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine. These include: * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February) * {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}} (June and December) * {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) Other rites such as {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}}, {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}}, National Foundation Day (February 11), {{ill|Kaze no Hi no Matsuri|ja|風日祈祭}} (May, August), and {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭}} (December 23) are conducted remotely.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.263 == Flora == Since it shares the grounds with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine, the plant life is identical. No parking is available. == Related Pages == * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|lt=Uji-no-Nuki Shrine|lt_ja=宇治乃奴鬼神社}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:国津御祖神社]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == 関連項目 == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0306.html 国津御祖神社（くにつみおやじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（葦立弖神社の説明を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|くにつみおやしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine-qq]] [[ja:国津御祖神社]][[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:ppp]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "倭姫命}}" to "倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}"
revision.text:
{{Shrine |name= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Kunitsu Mioya & Ashidate shrine.jpg|270px]] |location= {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg= 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.5 |longitude_deg= 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.4 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: Ujihime-no-Mikoto, Tamurahime-no-Mikoto
Ashidate Shrine: Tamairahime-no-Mikoto |rank= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} Subordinate Shrine
Ashidate Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |other_names= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: Mikosha (御子社) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }} '''Kunitsu Mioya Shrine''' (国津御祖神社, *Kunitsu Mioya Jinja*) is a {{ill|subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}). This article also covers '''Ashidate Shrine''' (葦立弖神社, *Ashidate Jinja*), which is co-located with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.107. [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[ja:国津御祖神社]] == Overview == {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社}} and {{ill|Ashitate Shrine|ja|葦立弖神社}} are located at 2132, Oazaki, Kusube-cho, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.273 They are situated further along the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine}} and {{ill|Uji no Nukigami Shrine|ja|宇治乃奴鬼神社}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.106-107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.260 The area spans approximately 1,226 m².Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.265 According to the *Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō*, these shrines have been located here since the Heian period. Both shrines share a single {{ill|Shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with wooden boards, facing south, and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.275 There is no offertory box. Historically, the building was thatched. == Kunitsu Mioya Shrine == Ranked 11th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Naikū|ja|内宮}}.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Uji-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇治比賣命}} and {{ill|Tamura-hime no Mikoto|ja|田村比賣命}}. Both are children of the {{ill|Kuninushi no Kami|ja|国生の神}} and are regarded as local tutelary deities. Due to their divine lineage, the shrine is also called "Miko-sha" (御子社), meaning "Shrine of the Divine Children."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.272 == Ashitate Shrine == Ranked 14th among the 16 subordinate shrines of Naikū.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine building no longer exists, and the deity is enshrined together with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.14 The enshrined deity is {{ill|Tamayara-hime no Mikoto|ja|玉移良比女命}}, considered a child of {{ill|Ujitsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇治都比女命}} and a local tutelary deity. == History == Established before 927 CE, as mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It is also listed in the *Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō*, confirming its existence before 804 CE. Worship ceased in the medieval period, but the shrines were restored on December 22, 1664 (Gregorian: January 20, 1665). Since the original site of Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine was easy to locate,Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.262 both shrines were re-established at their original locations. In 1871, Ashitate Shrine was formally merged with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine. The shrine building was reconstructed in 1914, rebuilt in 1959, and extensively renovated in 1982. == Rituals == Rituals are performed similarly to those at Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine. These include: * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February) * {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}} (June and December) * {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) Other rites such as {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}}, {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}}, National Foundation Day (February 11), {{ill|Kaze no Hi no Matsuri|ja|風日祈祭}} (May, August), and {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭}} (December 23) are conducted remotely.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.263 == Flora == Since it shares the grounds with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine, the plant life is identical. A 1977 survey found 258 trees, including Kaya, {{ill|Inumaki|ja|イヌマキ}}, Cryptomeria, Hinoki, Oak, Shii, Camphor, Yabu-nikkei, Shirodamo, Tabu, Tobera, Mochi, and Yabutsubaki. == Access == [[File:Chaya no Mori in Kusube.jpg|thumb|Entrance to Kunitsu Mioya Shrine and Ashitate Shrine]] * **By Train:** From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} (Kintetsu Toba Line) * 13-minute walk (~1 km) * Take the {{ill|Ise Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス}} "Okage Bus," alight at "Shigo Elementary School" stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m) * **By Car:** From {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}} (Ise Expressway), drive south on National Route 23, turn left at "Kusube Intersection," and continue east on Prefectural Route 37 for ~750 m. No parking is available. == Related Pages == * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|lt=Uji-no-Nuki Shrine|lt_ja=宇治乃奴鬼神社}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:国津御祖神社]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == 関連項目 == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0306.html 国津御祖神社（くにつみおやじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（葦立弖神社の説明を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|くにつみおやしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine-qq]] [[ja:国津御祖神社]][[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:ppp]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Kunitsu Mioya & Ashidate shrine.jpg|270px]] |location= {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg= 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.5 |longitude_deg= 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.4 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: Ujihime-no-Mikoto, Tamurahime-no-Mikoto
Ashidate Shrine: Tamairahime-no-Mikoto |rank= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} Subordinate Shrine
Ashidate Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |other_names= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: Mikosha (御子社) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }} '''Kunitsu Mioya Shrine''' (国津御祖神社, *Kunitsu Mioya Jinja*) is a {{ill|subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}). This article also covers '''Ashidate Shrine''' (葦立弖神社, *Ashidate Jinja*), which is co-located with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.107. [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[ja:国津御祖神社]] == Overview == {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社}} and {{ill|Ashitate Shrine|ja|葦立弖神社}} are located at 2132, Oazaki, Kusube-cho, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.273 They are situated further along the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine}} and {{ill|Uji no Nukigami Shrine|ja|宇治乃奴鬼神社}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.106-107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.260 The area spans approximately 1,226 m².Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.265 According to the *Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō*, these shrines have been located here since the Heian period. Both shrines share a single {{ill|Shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with wooden boards, facing south, and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.275 There is no offertory box. Historically, the building was thatched. == Kunitsu Mioya Shrine == Ranked 11th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Naikū|ja|内宮}}.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Uji-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇治比賣命}} and {{ill|Tamura-hime no Mikoto|ja|田村比賣命}}. Both are children of the {{ill|Kuninushi no Kami|ja|国生の神}} and are regarded as local tutelary deities. Due to their divine lineage, the shrine is also called "Miko-sha" (御子社), meaning "Shrine of the Divine Children."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.272 == Ashitate Shrine == Ranked 14th among the 16 subordinate shrines of Naikū.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine building no longer exists, and the deity is enshrined together with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.14 The enshrined deity is {{ill|Tamayara-hime no Mikoto|ja|玉移良比女命}}, considered a child of {{ill|Ujitsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇治都比女命}} and a local tutelary deity. == History == Established before 927 CE, as mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It is also listed in the *Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō*, confirming its existence before 804 CE. Worship ceased in the medieval period, but the shrines were restored on December 22, 1664 (Gregorian: January 20, 1665). Since the original site of Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine was easy to locate,Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.262 both shrines were re-established at their original locations. In 1871, Ashitate Shrine was formally merged with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine. The shrine building was reconstructed in 1914, rebuilt in 1959, and extensively renovated in 1982. == Rituals == Rituals are performed similarly to those at Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine. These include: * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February) * {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}} (June and December) * {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) Other rites such as {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}}, {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}}, National Foundation Day (February 11), {{ill|Kaze no Hi no Matsuri|ja|風日祈祭}} (May, August), and {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭}} (December 23) are conducted remotely.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.263 == Flora == Since it shares the grounds with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine, the plant life is identical. A 1977 survey found 258 trees, including Kaya, {{ill|Inumaki|ja|イヌマキ}}, Cryptomeria, Hinoki, Oak, Shii, Camphor, Yabu-nikkei, Shirodamo, Tabu, Tobera, Mochi, and Yabutsubaki. == Access == [[File:Chaya no Mori in Kusube.jpg|thumb|Entrance to Kunitsu Mioya Shrine and Ashitate Shrine]] * **By Train:** From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} (Kintetsu Toba Line) * 13-minute walk (~1 km) * Take the {{ill|Ise Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス}} "Okage Bus," alight at "Shigo Elementary School" stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m) * **By Car:** From {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}} (Ise Expressway), drive south on National Route 23, turn left at "Kusube Intersection," and continue east on Prefectural Route 37 for ~750 m. No parking is available. == Related Pages == * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|lt=Uji-no-Nuki Shrine|lt_ja=宇治乃奴鬼神社}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:国津御祖神社]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0306.html 国津御祖神社（くにつみおやじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（葦立弖神社の説明を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|くにつみおやしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine-qq]] [[ja:国津御祖神社]][[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:国津御祖神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:ppp]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]]
