revision.text:
{{Shrine |name= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Kunitsu Mioya & Ashidate shrine.jpg|270px]] |location= {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft}}, Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg= 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.5 |longitude_deg= 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.4 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: Ujihime-no-Mikoto, Tamurahime-no-Mikoto
Ashidate Shrine: Tamairahime-no-Mikoto |Rank= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft}} Subordinate Shrine
Ashidate Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft}} |other_names= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: Mikosha (御子社) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft}} }} '''Kunitsu Mioya Shrine''' (国津御祖神社, *Kunitsu Mioya Jinja*) is a {{ill|subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft}} (the Inner Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft}}). This article also covers '''Ashidate Shrine''' (葦立弖神社, *Ashidate Jinja*), which is co-located with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.107. == Overview == {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Ashitate Shrine|ja|葦立弖神社|qq=draft}} are located at 2132, Oazaki, Kusube-cho, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.273 They are situated further along the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Uji no Nukigami Shrine|ja|宇治乃奴鬼神社|qq=draft}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.106-107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.260 The area spans approximately 1,226 m².Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.265 According to the *Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō*, these shrines have been located here since the Heian period. Both shrines share a single {{ill|Shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft}} style with wooden boards, facing south, and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.275 There is no offertory box. Historically, the building was thatched. == Kunitsu Mioya Shrine == Ranked 11th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Naikū|ja|内宮|qq=draft}}.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Uji-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇治比賣命|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Tamura-hime no Mikoto|ja|田村比賣命|qq=draft}}. Both are children of the {{ill|Kuninushi no Kami|ja|国生の神|qq=draft}} and are regarded as local tutelary deities. Due to their divine lineage, the shrine is also called "Miko-sha" (御子社), meaning "Shrine of the Divine Children."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.272 == Ashitate Shrine == Ranked 14th among the 16 subordinate shrines of Naikū.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine building no longer exists, and the deity is enshrined together with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.14 The enshrined deity is {{ill|Tamayara-hime no Mikoto|ja|玉移良比女命|qq=draft}}, considered a child of {{ill|Ujitsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇治都比女命|qq=draft}} and a local tutelary deity. == History == Established before 927 CE, as mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It is also listed in the *Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō*, confirming its existence before 804 CE. Worship ceased in the medieval period, but the shrines were restored on December 22, 1664 (Gregorian: January 20, 1665). Since the original site of Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine was easy to locate,Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.262 both shrines were re-established at their original locations. In 1871, Ashitate Shrine was formally merged with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine. The shrine building was reconstructed in 1914, rebuilt in 1959, and extensively renovated in 1982. == Rituals == Rituals are performed similarly to those at Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine. These include: * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft}} (February) * {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft}} (June and December) * {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft}} (October) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft}} (November) Other rites such as {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft}}, National Foundation Day (February 11), {{ill|Kaze no Hi no Matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|qq=draft}} (May, August), and {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|qq=draft}} (December 23) are conducted remotely.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.263 == Flora == Since it shares the grounds with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine, the plant life is identical. A 1977 survey found 258 trees, including Kaya, {{ill|Inumaki|ja|イヌマキ|qq=draft}}, Cryptomeria, Hinoki, Oak, Shii, Camphor, Yabu-nikkei, Shirodamo, Tabu, Tobera, Mochi, and Yabutsubaki. == Access == [[File:Chaya no Mori in Kusube.jpg|thumb|Entrance to Kunitsu Mioya Shrine and Ashitate Shrine]] * **By Train:** From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅|qq=draft}} (Kintetsu Toba Line) ** 13-minute walk (~1 km) ** Take the {{ill|Ise Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス|qq=draft}} "Okage Bus," alight at "Shigo Elementary School" stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m) * **By Car:** From {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|qq=draft}} (Ise Expressway), drive south on National Route 23, turn left at "Kusube Intersection," and continue east on Prefectural Route 37 for ~750 m. No parking is available. == Related Pages == * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|lt=Uji-no-Nuki Shrine|lt_ja=宇治乃奴鬼神社|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0306.html 国津御祖神社（くにつみおやじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（葦立弖神社の説明を含む） {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|国津御祖神社|version=64047304|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:国津御祖神社]] [[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]] [[sn:unitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]]

Historically, the building was thatched. == Kunitsu Mioya Shrine == Ranked 11th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Naikū|ja|内宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikū|qq=}}.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Uji-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇治比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji-hime no Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Tamura-hime no Mikoto|ja|田村比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}. Both are children of the {{ill|Kuninushi no Kami|ja|国生の神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninushi no Kami|qq=}} and are regarded as local tutelary deities. Due to their divine lineage, the shrine is also called "Miko-sha" (御子社), meaning "Shrine of the Divine Children."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.272 == Ashitate Shrine == Ranked 14th among the 16 subordinate shrines of Naikū.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine building no longer exists, and the deity is enshrined together with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.14 The enshrined deity is {{ill|Tamayara-hime no Mikoto|ja|玉移良比女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayara-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}, considered a child of {{ill|Ujitsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇治都比女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitsu-hime no Mikoto|qq=}} and a local tutelary deity. == History == Established before 927 CE, as mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It is also listed in the *Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō*, confirming its existence before 804 CE. Worship ceased in the medieval period, but the shrines were restored on December 22, 1664 (Gregorian: January 20, 1665). Since the original site of Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine was easy to locate,Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.262 both shrines were re-established at their original locations. In 1871, Ashitate Shrine was formally merged with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine. The shrine building was reconstructed in 1914, rebuilt in 1959, and extensively renovated in 1982. == Rituals == Rituals are performed similarly to those at Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine. These include: * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinensai|qq=}} (February) * {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June and December) * {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} (October) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=}} (November) Other rites such as {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}}, {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}}, National Foundation Day (February 11), {{ill|Kaze no Hi no Matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze no Hi no Matsuri|qq=}} (May, August), and {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|qq=}} (December 23) are conducted remotely.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.263 == Flora == Since it shares the grounds with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine, the plant life is identical. A 1977 survey found 258 trees, including Kaya, {{ill|Inumaki|ja|イヌマキ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inumaki|qq=}}, Cryptomeria, Hinoki, Oak, Shii, Camphor, Yabu-nikkei, Shirodamo, Tabu, Tobera, Mochi, and Yabutsubaki. == Access == [[File:Chaya no Mori in Kusube.jpg|thumb|Entrance to Kunitsu Mioya Shrine and Ashitate Shrine]] * **By Train:** From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzugawa Station|qq=}} (Kintetsu Toba Line) ** 13-minute walk (~1 km) ** Take the {{ill|Ise Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Community Bus|qq=}} "Okage Bus," alight at "Shigo Elementary School" stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m) * **By Car:** From {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Interchange|qq=}} (Ise Expressway), drive south on National Route 23, turn left at "Kusube Intersection," and continue east on Prefectural Route 37 for ~750 m. No parking is available. == Related Pages == * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|lt=Uji-no-Nuki Shrine|lt_ja=宇治乃奴鬼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0306.html 国津御祖神社（くにつみおやじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（葦立弖神社の説明を含む） {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|国津御祖神社|version=64047304|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:国津御祖神社]] [[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]] [[sn:unitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Kunitsu Mioya & Ashidate shrine.jpg|270px]] |location= {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise, Mie|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}, Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg= 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.5 |longitude_deg= 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.4 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: Ujihime-no-Mikoto, Tamurahime-no-Mikoto
Ashidate Shrine: Tamairahime-no-Mikoto |Rank= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} Subordinate Shrine
Ashidate Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} |other_names= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: Mikosha (御子社) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} }} '''Kunitsu Mioya Shrine''' (国津御祖神社, *Kunitsu Mioya Jinja*) is a {{ill|subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)|ja|摂末社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)|qq=}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (the Inner Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}). This article also covers '''Ashidate Shrine''' (葦立弖神社, *Ashidate Jinja*), which is co-located with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.107. == Overview == {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Mioya Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Ashitate Shrine|ja|葦立弖神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashitate Shrine|qq=}} are located at 2132, Oazaki, Kusube-cho, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.273 They are situated further along the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Uji no Nukigami Shrine|ja|宇治乃奴鬼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Nukigami Shrine|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.106-107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.260 The area spans approximately 1,226 m².Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.265 According to the *Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō*, these shrines have been located here since the Heian period. Both shrines share a single {{ill|Shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaden (shrine building)|qq=}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} style with wooden boards, facing south, and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagaki|qq=}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.275 There is no offertory box. Historically, the building was thatched. == Kunitsu Mioya Shrine == Ranked 11th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Naikū|ja|内宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikū|qq=}}.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Uji-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇治比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji-hime no Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Tamura-hime no Mikoto|ja|田村比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}. Both are children of the {{ill|Kuninushi no Kami|ja|国生の神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninushi no Kami|qq=}} and are regarded as local tutelary deities. Due to their divine lineage, the shrine is also called "Miko-sha" (御子社), meaning "Shrine of the Divine Children."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.272 == Ashitate Shrine == Ranked 14th among the 16 subordinate shrines of Naikū.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine building no longer exists, and the deity is enshrined together with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.14 The enshrined deity is {{ill|Tamayara-hime no Mikoto|ja|玉移良比女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayara-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}, considered a child of {{ill|Ujitsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇治都比女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitsu-hime no Mikoto|qq=}} and a local tutelary deity. == History == Established before 927 CE, as mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It is also listed in the *Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō*, confirming its existence before 804 CE. Worship ceased in the medieval period, but the shrines were restored on December 22, 1664 (Gregorian: January 20, 1665). Since the original site of Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine was easy to locate,Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.262 both shrines were re-established at their original locations. In 1871, Ashitate Shrine was formally merged with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine. The shrine building was reconstructed in 1914, rebuilt in 1959, and extensively renovated in 1982. == Rituals == Rituals are performed similarly to those at Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine. These include: * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinensai|qq=}} (February) * {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June and December) * {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} (October) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=}} (November) Other rites such as {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}}, {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}}, National Foundation Day (February 11), {{ill|Kaze no Hi no Matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze no Hi no Matsuri|qq=}} (May, August), and {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|qq=}} (December 23) are conducted remotely.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.263 == Flora == Since it shares the grounds with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine, the plant life is identical. A 1977 survey found 258 trees, including Kaya, {{ill|Inumaki|ja|イヌマキ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inumaki|qq=}}, Cryptomeria, Hinoki, Oak, Shii, Camphor, Yabu-nikkei, Shirodamo, Tabu, Tobera, Mochi, and Yabutsubaki. == Access == [[File:Chaya no Mori in Kusube.jpg|thumb|Entrance to Kunitsu Mioya Shrine and Ashitate Shrine]] * **By Train:** From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzugawa Station|qq=}} (Kintetsu Toba Line) ** 13-minute walk (~1 km) ** Take the {{ill|Ise Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Community Bus|qq=}} "Okage Bus," alight at "Shigo Elementary School" stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m) * **By Car:** From {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Interchange|qq=}} (Ise Expressway), drive south on National Route 23, turn left at "Kusube Intersection," and continue east on Prefectural Route 37 for ~750 m. No parking is available. == Related Pages == * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|lt=Uji-no-Nuki Shrine|lt_ja=宇治乃奴鬼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0306.html 国津御祖神社（くにつみおやじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（葦立弖神社の説明を含む） {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|国津御祖神社|version=64047304|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:国津御祖神社]] [[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]] [[sn:unitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]]

{{Shrine |name= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Kunitsu Mioya & Ashidate shrine.jpg|270px]] |location= {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise, Mie|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}, Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg= 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.5 |longitude_deg= 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.4 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: Ujihime-no-Mikoto, Tamurahime-no-Mikoto
Ashidate Shrine: Tamairahime-no-Mikoto |Rank= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} Subordinate Shrine
Ashidate Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} |other_names= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: Mikosha (御子社) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} }} '''Kunitsu Mioya Shrine''' (国津御祖神社, *Kunitsu Mioya Jinja*) is a {{ill|subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)|ja|摂末社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)|qq=}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (the Inner Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}). This article also covers '''Ashidate Shrine''' (葦立弖神社, *Ashidate Jinja*), which is co-located with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.107. == Overview == {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Mioya Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Ashitate Shrine|ja|葦立弖神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashitate Shrine|qq=}} are located at 2132, Oazaki, Kusube-cho, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.273 They are situated further along the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Uji no Nukigami Shrine|ja|宇治乃奴鬼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Nukigami Shrine|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.106-107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.260 The area spans approximately 1,226 m².Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.265 According to the *Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō*, these shrines have been located here since the Heian period. Both shrines share a single {{ill|Shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaden (shrine building)|qq=}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} style with wooden boards, facing south, and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagaki|qq=}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.275 There is no offertory box. Historically, the building was thatched. == Kunitsu Mioya Shrine == Ranked 11th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Naikū|ja|内宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikū|qq=}}.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Uji-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇治比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji-hime no Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Tamura-hime no Mikoto|ja|田村比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}. Both are children of the {{ill|Kuninushi no Kami|ja|国生の神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninushi no Kami|qq=}} and are regarded as local tutelary deities. Due to their divine lineage, the shrine is also called "Miko-sha" (御子社), meaning "Shrine of the Divine Children."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.272 == Ashitate Shrine == Ranked 14th among the 16 subordinate shrines of Naikū.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine building no longer exists, and the deity is enshrined together with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.14 The enshrined deity is {{ill|Tamayara-hime no Mikoto|ja|玉移良比女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayara-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}, considered a child of {{ill|Ujitsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇治都比女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitsu-hime no Mikoto|qq=}} and a local tutelary deity. == History == Established before 927 CE, as mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It is also listed in the *Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō*, confirming its existence before 804 CE. Worship ceased in the medieval period, but the shrines were restored on December 22, 1664 (Gregorian: January 20, 1665). Since the original site of Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine was easy to locate,Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.262 both shrines were re-established at their original locations. In 1871, Ashitate Shrine was formally merged with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine. The shrine building was reconstructed in 1914, rebuilt in 1959, and extensively renovated in 1982. == Rituals == Rituals are performed similarly to those at Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine. These include: * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinensai|qq=}} (February) * {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June and December) * {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} (October) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=}} (November) Other rites such as {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}}, {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}}, National Foundation Day (February 11), {{ill|Kaze no Hi no Matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze no Hi no Matsuri|qq=}} (May, August), and {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|qq=}} (December 23) are conducted remotely.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.263 == Flora == Since it shares the grounds with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine, the plant life is identical. A 1977 survey found 258 trees, including Kaya, {{ill|Inumaki|ja|イヌマキ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inumaki|qq=}}, Cryptomeria, Hinoki, Oak, Shii, Camphor, Yabu-nikkei, Shirodamo, Tabu, Tobera, Mochi, and Yabutsubaki. == Access == [[File:Chaya no Mori in Kusube.jpg|thumb|Entrance to Kunitsu Mioya Shrine and Ashitate Shrine]] * **By Train:** From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzugawa Station|qq=}} (Kintetsu Toba Line) ** 13-minute walk (~1 km) ** Take the {{ill|Ise Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Community Bus|qq=}} "Okage Bus," alight at "Shigo Elementary School" stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m) * **By Car:** From {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Interchange|qq=}} (Ise Expressway), drive south on National Route 23, turn left at "Kusube Intersection," and continue east on Prefectural Route 37 for ~750 m. No parking is available. == Related Pages == * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|lt=Uji-no-Nuki Shrine|lt_ja=宇治乃奴鬼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0306.html 国津御祖神社（くにつみおやじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（葦立弖神社の説明を含む） {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|国津御祖神社|version=64047304|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:国津御祖神社]] [[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]] [[sn:unitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]]

Both shrines share a single {{ill|Shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaden (shrine building)|qq=}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} style with wooden boards, facing south, and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagaki|qq=}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.275 There is no offertory box. Historically, the building was thatched. == Kunitsu Mioya Shrine == Ranked 11th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Naikū|ja|内宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikū|qq=}}.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Uji-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇治比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji-hime no Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Tamura-hime no Mikoto|ja|田村比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}. Both are children of the {{ill|Kuninushi no Kami|ja|国生の神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninushi no Kami|qq=}} and are regarded as local tutelary deities. Due to their divine lineage, the shrine is also called "Miko-sha" (御子社), meaning "Shrine of the Divine Children."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.272 == Ashitate Shrine == Ranked 14th among the 16 subordinate shrines of Naikū.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine building no longer exists, and the deity is enshrined together with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.14 The enshrined deity is {{ill|Tamayara-hime no Mikoto|ja|玉移良比女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayara-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}, considered a child of {{ill|Ujitsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇治都比女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitsu-hime no Mikoto|qq=}} and a local tutelary deity. == History == Established before 927 CE, as mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It is also listed in the *Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō*, confirming its existence before 804 CE. Worship ceased in the medieval period, but the shrines were restored on December 22, 1664 (Gregorian: January 20, 1665). Since the original site of Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine was easy to locate,Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.262 both shrines were re-established at their original locations. In 1871, Ashitate Shrine was formally merged with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine. The shrine building was reconstructed in 1914, rebuilt in 1959, and extensively renovated in 1982. == Rituals == Rituals are performed similarly to those at Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine. These include: * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinensai|qq=}} (February) * {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June and December) * {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} (October) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=}} (November) Other rites such as {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}}, {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}}, National Foundation Day (February 11), {{ill|Kaze no Hi no Matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze no Hi no Matsuri|qq=}} (May, August), and {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|qq=}} (December 23) are conducted remotely.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.263 == Flora == Since it shares the grounds with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine, the plant life is identical. A 1977 survey found 258 trees, including Kaya, {{ill|Inumaki|ja|イヌマキ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inumaki|qq=}}, Cryptomeria, Hinoki, Oak, Shii, Camphor, Yabu-nikkei, Shirodamo, Tabu, Tobera, Mochi, and Yabutsubaki. == Access == [[File:Chaya no Mori in Kusube.jpg|thumb|Entrance to Kunitsu Mioya Shrine and Ashitate Shrine]] * **By Train:** From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzugawa Station|qq=}} (Kintetsu Toba Line) ** 13-minute walk (~1 km) ** Take the {{ill|Ise Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Community Bus|qq=}} "Okage Bus," alight at "Shigo Elementary School" stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m) * **By Car:** From {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Interchange|qq=}} (Ise Expressway), drive south on National Route 23, turn left at "Kusube Intersection," and continue east on Prefectural Route 37 for ~750 m. No parking is available. == Related Pages == * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|lt=Uji-no-Nuki Shrine|lt_ja=宇治乃奴鬼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0306.html 国津御祖神社（くにつみおやじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（葦立弖神社の説明を含む） {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|国津御祖神社|version=64047304|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:国津御祖神社]] [[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]] [[sn:unitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]]

revision.comment: Bot: fix redirect [[Niinamesai]] → [[Niiname-no-Matsuri]]
revision.text:
{{Shrine |name= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Kunitsu Mioya & Ashidate shrine.jpg|270px]] |location= {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise, Mie|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}, Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg= 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.5 |longitude_deg= 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.4 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: Ujihime-no-Mikoto, Tamurahime-no-Mikoto
Ashidate Shrine: Tamairahime-no-Mikoto |Rank= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} Subordinate Shrine
Ashidate Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} |other_names= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: Mikosha (御子社) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} }} '''Kunitsu Mioya Shrine''' (国津御祖神社, *Kunitsu Mioya Jinja*) is a {{ill|subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)|ja|摂末社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)|qq=}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (the Inner Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}). This article also covers '''Ashidate Shrine''' (葦立弖神社, *Ashidate Jinja*), which is co-located with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.107. == Overview == {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Mioya Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Ashitate Shrine|ja|葦立弖神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashitate Shrine|qq=}} are located at 2132, Oazaki, Kusube-cho, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.273 They are situated further along the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Uji no Nukigami Shrine|ja|宇治乃奴鬼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Nukigami Shrine|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.106-107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.260 The area spans approximately 1,226 m².Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.265 According to the *Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō*, these shrines have been located here since the Heian period. Both shrines share a single {{ill|Shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaden (shrine building)|qq=}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} style with wooden boards, facing south, and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagaki|qq=}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.275 There is no offertory box. Historically, the building was thatched. == Kunitsu Mioya Shrine == Ranked 11th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Naikū|ja|内宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikū|qq=}}.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Uji-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇治比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji-hime no Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Tamura-hime no Mikoto|ja|田村比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}. Both are children of the {{ill|Kuninushi no Kami|ja|国生の神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninushi no Kami|qq=}} and are regarded as local tutelary deities. Due to their divine lineage, the shrine is also called "Miko-sha" (御子社), meaning "Shrine of the Divine Children."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.272 == Ashitate Shrine == Ranked 14th among the 16 subordinate shrines of Naikū.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine building no longer exists, and the deity is enshrined together with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.14 The enshrined deity is {{ill|Tamayara-hime no Mikoto|ja|玉移良比女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayara-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}, considered a child of {{ill|Ujitsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇治都比女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitsu-hime no Mikoto|qq=}} and a local tutelary deity. == History == Established before 927 CE, as mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It is also listed in the *Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō*, confirming its existence before 804 CE. Worship ceased in the medieval period, but the shrines were restored on December 22, 1664 (Gregorian: January 20, 1665). Since the original site of Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine was easy to locate,Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.262 both shrines were re-established at their original locations. In 1871, Ashitate Shrine was formally merged with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine. The shrine building was reconstructed in 1914, rebuilt in 1959, and extensively renovated in 1982. == Rituals == Rituals are performed similarly to those at Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine. These include: * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinensai|qq=}} (February) * {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June and December) * {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} (October) * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=}} (November) Other rites such as {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}}, {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}}, National Foundation Day (February 11), {{ill|Kaze no Hi no Matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze no Hi no Matsuri|qq=}} (May, August), and {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|qq=}} (December 23) are conducted remotely.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.263 == Flora == Since it shares the grounds with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine, the plant life is identical. A 1977 survey found 258 trees, including Kaya, {{ill|Inumaki|ja|イヌマキ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inumaki|qq=}}, Cryptomeria, Hinoki, Oak, Shii, Camphor, Yabu-nikkei, Shirodamo, Tabu, Tobera, Mochi, and Yabutsubaki. == Access == [[File:Chaya no Mori in Kusube.jpg|thumb|Entrance to Kunitsu Mioya Shrine and Ashitate Shrine]] * **By Train:** From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzugawa Station|qq=}} (Kintetsu Toba Line) ** 13-minute walk (~1 km) ** Take the {{ill|Ise Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Community Bus|qq=}} "Okage Bus," alight at "Shigo Elementary School" stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m) * **By Car:** From {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Interchange|qq=}} (Ise Expressway), drive south on National Route 23, turn left at "Kusube Intersection," and continue east on Prefectural Route 37 for ~750 m. No parking is available. == Related Pages == * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|lt=Uji-no-Nuki Shrine|lt_ja=宇治乃奴鬼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0306.html 国津御祖神社（くにつみおやじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（葦立弖神社の説明を含む） {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|国津御祖神社|version=64047304|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:国津御祖神社]] [[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]] [[sn:unitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]]

Both shrines share a single {{ill|Shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaden (shrine building)|qq=}} constructed in [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with wooden boards, facing south, and enclosed by a single [[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki]].Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.275 There is no offertory box. Historically, the building was thatched. == Kunitsu Mioya Shrine == Ranked 11th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Naikū|ja|内宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikū|qq=}}.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Uji-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇治比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji-hime no Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Tamura-hime no Mikoto|ja|田村比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}. Both are children of the {{ill|Kuninushi no Kami|ja|国生の神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninushi no Kami|qq=}} and are regarded as local tutelary deities. Due to their divine lineage, the shrine is also called "Miko-sha" (御子社), meaning "Shrine of the Divine Children."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.272 == Ashitate Shrine == Ranked 14th among the 16 subordinate shrines of Naikū.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine building no longer exists, and the deity is enshrined together with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.14 The enshrined deity is {{ill|Tamayara-hime no Mikoto|ja|玉移良比女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayara-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}, considered a child of {{ill|Ujitsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇治都比女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitsu-hime no Mikoto|qq=}} and a local tutelary deity. == History == Established before 927 CE, as mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It is also listed in the *Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō*, confirming its existence before 804 CE. Worship ceased in the medieval period, but the shrines were restored on December 22, 1664 (Gregorian: January 20, 1665). Since the original site of Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine was easy to locate,Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.262 both shrines were re-established at their original locations. In 1871, Ashitate Shrine was formally merged with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine. The shrine building was reconstructed in 1914, rebuilt in 1959, and extensively renovated in 1982. == Rituals == Rituals are performed similarly to those at Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine. These include: * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (February) * {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June and December) * [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October) * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (November) Other rites such as {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}}, {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}}, National Foundation Day (February 11), {{ill|Kaze no Hi no Matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze no Hi no Matsuri|qq=}} (May, August), and {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|qq=}} (December 23) are conducted remotely.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.263 == Flora == Since it shares the grounds with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine, the plant life is identical. A 1977 survey found 258 trees, including Kaya, [[:en:Podocarpus macrophyllus|Inumaki]], Cryptomeria, Hinoki, Oak, Shii, Camphor, Yabu-nikkei, Shirodamo, Tabu, Tobera, Mochi, and Yabutsubaki. == Access == [[File:Chaya no Mori in Kusube.jpg|thumb|Entrance to Kunitsu Mioya Shrine and Ashitate Shrine]] * **By Train:** From [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] (Kintetsu Toba Line) ** 13-minute walk (~1 km) ** Take the {{ill|Ise Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Community Bus|qq=}} "Okage Bus," alight at "Shigo Elementary School" stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m) * **By Car:** From {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Interchange|qq=}} (Ise Expressway), drive south on National Route 23, turn left at "Kusube Intersection," and continue east on Prefectural Route 37 for ~750 m. No parking is available. == Related Pages == * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|lt=Uji-no-Nuki Shrine|lt_ja=宇治乃奴鬼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0306.html 国津御祖神社（くにつみおやじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（葦立弖神社の説明を含む） {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|国津御祖神社|version=64047304|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:国津御祖神社]] [[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]] [[sn:unitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]]

{{Shrine |name= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Kunitsu Mioya & Ashidate shrine.jpg|270px]] |location= [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg= 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.5 |longitude_deg= 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.4 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: Ujihime-no-Mikoto, Tamurahime-no-Mikoto
Ashidate Shrine: Tamairahime-no-Mikoto |Rank= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} Subordinate Shrine
Ashidate Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |other_names= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: Mikosha (御子社) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] }} '''Kunitsu Mioya Shrine''' (国津御祖神社, *Kunitsu Mioya Jinja*) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (the Inner Shrine of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]). This article also covers '''Ashidate Shrine''' (葦立弖神社, *Ashidate Jinja*), which is co-located with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.107. == Overview == {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Mioya Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Ashitate Shrine|ja|葦立弖神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashitate Shrine|qq=}} are located at 2132, Oazaki, Kusube-cho, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.273 They are situated further along the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Uji no Nukigami Shrine|ja|宇治乃奴鬼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Nukigami Shrine|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.106-107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.260 The area spans approximately 1,226 m².Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.265 According to the *Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō*, these shrines have been located here since the Heian period. Both shrines share a single {{ill|Shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaden (shrine building)|qq=}} constructed in [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with wooden boards, facing south, and enclosed by a single [[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki]].Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.275 There is no offertory box. Historically, the building was thatched. == Kunitsu Mioya Shrine == Ranked 11th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Naikū|ja|内宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikū|qq=}}.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Uji-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇治比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji-hime no Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Tamura-hime no Mikoto|ja|田村比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}. Both are children of the {{ill|Kuninushi no Kami|ja|国生の神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninushi no Kami|qq=}} and are regarded as local tutelary deities. Due to their divine lineage, the shrine is also called "Miko-sha" (御子社), meaning "Shrine of the Divine Children."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.272 == Ashitate Shrine == Ranked 14th among the 16 subordinate shrines of Naikū.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine building no longer exists, and the deity is enshrined together with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.14 The enshrined deity is {{ill|Tamayara-hime no Mikoto|ja|玉移良比女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayara-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}, considered a child of {{ill|Ujitsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇治都比女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitsu-hime no Mikoto|qq=}} and a local tutelary deity. == History == Established before 927 CE, as mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It is also listed in the *Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō*, confirming its existence before 804 CE. Worship ceased in the medieval period, but the shrines were restored on December 22, 1664 (Gregorian: January 20, 1665). Since the original site of Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine was easy to locate,Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.262 both shrines were re-established at their original locations. In 1871, Ashitate Shrine was formally merged with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine. The shrine building was reconstructed in 1914, rebuilt in 1959, and extensively renovated in 1982. == Rituals == Rituals are performed similarly to those at Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine. These include: * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (February) * {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June and December) * [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October) * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (November) Other rites such as {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}}, {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}}, National Foundation Day (February 11), {{ill|Kaze no Hi no Matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze no Hi no Matsuri|qq=}} (May, August), and {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|qq=}} (December 23) are conducted remotely.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.263 == Flora == Since it shares the grounds with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine, the plant life is identical. A 1977 survey found 258 trees, including Kaya, [[:en:Podocarpus macrophyllus|Inumaki]], Cryptomeria, Hinoki, Oak, Shii, Camphor, Yabu-nikkei, Shirodamo, Tabu, Tobera, Mochi, and Yabutsubaki. == Access == [[File:Chaya no Mori in Kusube.jpg|thumb|Entrance to Kunitsu Mioya Shrine and Ashitate Shrine]] * **By Train:** From [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] (Kintetsu Toba Line) ** 13-minute walk (~1 km) ** Take the {{ill|Ise Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Community Bus|qq=}} "Okage Bus," alight at "Shigo Elementary School" stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m) * **By Car:** From {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Interchange|qq=}} (Ise Expressway), drive south on National Route 23, turn left at "Kusube Intersection," and continue east on Prefectural Route 37 for ~750 m. No parking is available. == Related Pages == * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|lt=Uji-no-Nuki Shrine|lt_ja=宇治乃奴鬼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0306.html 国津御祖神社（くにつみおやじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（葦立弖神社の説明を含む） {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|国津御祖神社|version=64047304|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:国津御祖神社]] [[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]] [[sn:unitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]]

This article also covers '''Ashidate Shrine''' (葦立弖神社, *Ashidate Jinja*), which is co-located with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.107. == Overview == {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Mioya Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Ashitate Shrine|ja|葦立弖神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashitate Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国津御祖神社}} are located at 2132, Oazaki, Kusube-cho, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.273 They are situated further along the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Uji no Nukigami Shrine|ja|宇治乃奴鬼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Nukigami Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大土御祖神社}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.106-107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.260 The area spans approximately 1,226 m².Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.265 According to the *Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō*, these shrines have been located here since the Heian period. Both shrines share a single {{ill|Shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaden (shrine building)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築}} constructed in [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with wooden boards, facing south, and enclosed by a single [[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki]].Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.275 There is no offertory box. Historically, the building was thatched. == Kunitsu Mioya Shrine == Ranked 11th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Naikū|ja|内宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikū|qq=}}.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Uji-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇治比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji-hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Tamura-hime no Mikoto|ja|田村比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura-hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Both are children of the {{ill|Kuninushi no Kami|ja|国生の神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninushi no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and are regarded as local tutelary deities. Due to their divine lineage, the shrine is also called "Miko-sha" (御子社), meaning "Shrine of the Divine Children."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.272 == Ashitate Shrine == Ranked 14th among the 16 subordinate shrines of Naikū.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine building no longer exists, and the deity is enshrined together with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.14 The enshrined deity is {{ill|Tamayara-hime no Mikoto|ja|玉移良比女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayara-hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered a child of {{ill|Ujitsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇治都比女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitsu-hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a local tutelary deity. == History == Established before 927 CE, as mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It is also listed in the *Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō*, confirming its existence before 804 CE. Worship ceased in the medieval period, but the shrines were restored on December 22, 1664 (Gregorian: January 20, 1665). Since the original site of Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine was easy to locate,Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.262 both shrines were re-established at their original locations. In 1871, Ashitate Shrine was formally merged with Kunitsu Mioya Shrine. The shrine building was reconstructed in 1914, rebuilt in 1959, and extensively renovated in 1982. == Rituals == Rituals are performed similarly to those at Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine. These include: * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (February) * {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June and December) * [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October) * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (November) Other rites such as {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}}, {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}}, National Foundation Day (February 11), {{ill|Kaze no Hi no Matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze no Hi no Matsuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (May, August), and {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日}} (December 23) are conducted remotely.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.263 == Flora == Since it shares the grounds with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine, the plant life is identical. A 1977 survey found 258 trees, including Kaya, [[:en:Podocarpus macrophyllus|Inumaki]], Cryptomeria, Hinoki, Oak, Shii, Camphor, Yabu-nikkei, Shirodamo, Tabu, Tobera, Mochi, and Yabutsubaki. == Access == [[File:Chaya no Mori in Kusube.jpg|thumb|Entrance to Kunitsu Mioya Shrine and Ashitate Shrine]] * **By Train:** From [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] (Kintetsu Toba Line) ** 13-minute walk (~1 km) ** Take the {{ill|Ise Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Community Bus|qq=}} "Okage Bus," alight at "Shigo Elementary School" stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m) * **By Car:** From {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Interchange|qq=}} (Ise Expressway), drive south on National Route 23, turn left at "Kusube Intersection," and continue east on Prefectural Route 37 for ~750 m. No parking is available. == Related Pages == * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|lt=Uji-no-Nuki Shrine|lt_ja=宇治乃奴鬼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0306.html 国津御祖神社（くにつみおやじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（葦立弖神社の説明を含む） {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|国津御祖神社|version=64047304|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:国津御祖神社]] [[sn:Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]] [[sn:unitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine]]

Ashidate Shrine: Tamairahime-no-Mikoto |Rank= Kunitsu Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Subordinate Shrine
